source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 9. TCF_ADD(HAD1417MCW) 221215 (Rev.4) - PMS Update/Attachment 10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-2 Clinical Evaluation Report/Clinical Evaluation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-060, rev.1).docx

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
standards: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical Evaluation Report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector supporting safety, performance, equivalence, and benefit/risk conclusions under MDD clinical evaluation expectations.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd. as part of clinical evaluation and conformity assessment documentation.
keyPoints: ADD is described as a wired or wireless digital flat panel X-ray detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX of MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC.
The report states that clinical evaluation used literature review, equivalent predicate device data, and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.
The report concludes that the risks associated with use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

concepts.name: Clinical Evaluation Report
concepts.evidence: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

concepts.name: Intended Use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: MDD Class IIa Classification
concepts.evidence: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

concepts.name: Demonstration of Equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post Market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: PMS reports, including vigilance reports and trend reports

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: Through continuous PMS activity, the influence of the clinical performance and safety related to the device is constantly grasped in various aspects. In addition, PMCF studies will be conducted in accordance with the PMCF Plan if necessary.

concepts.name: Benefit/risk profile
concepts.evidence: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

concepts.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
concepts.evidence: The ADD is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector that can generate images of any part of the body and designed for a faster approach to digital radiography systems.

concepts.name: EN ISO 14971:2019 Risk Management
concepts.evidence: this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: The document is a Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision shown in the source text is A-TCF-5-060 Rev.2.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-060 (Rev.2)

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on 2022.12.01.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Reviewed by Approved by Name Kim Wan Su Lee, Dong jin Hur Namkyu Position R&D team R&D Manager Quality Manager Date 2022.12.01 2022.12.01 2022.12.01
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical Evaluation Report Dates And Signatures

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co.,Ltd. is the manufacturer of the device.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd.

evidence.claim: ADD is classified as Class IIa under Rule 16 of Annex IX of MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: The device intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The IFU-listed contraindications include not using the device for mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Patient Population A Adult and children except infant and pregnant women

evidence.claim: The target user group includes radiologists and users able to read and understand the manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Target User Group A a) Education: at least have the ability to read the manual b) Knowledge: User must be read and understand user manual. d) Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: The ADD uses a-Si TFT sensor technology and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI

evidence.claim: The ADD supports IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n Wi-Fi and 1Giga-bit Ethernet communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Wi-Fi IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n Communication 1Giga-bit Ethernet

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation considered MDD, RED, EN ISO, EN, IEC-related medical electrical and imaging standards, MEDDEV clinical evaluation, and PMCF guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: In the course of this clinical evaluation and considering the particular technical and clinical properties of the medical device to be evaluated, the following standards and guidelines are also taken into consideration:

evidence.claim: The report states the clinical evaluation was performed using literature review, equivalent predicate device data, and PMS data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation includes the overall results of literature review, extensive clinical data for substantially equivalent predicate device and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation is described as part of the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: The report assesses conformity to MDD Essential Requirements 1, 3, and 6.
evidence.evidenceText: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed: Essential Requirement 1: Safety Requirements Essential Requirement 3: Performance Requirements Essential Requirement 6: Side-effect Requirements

evidence.claim: The report states ADD was licensed or approved in South Korea, EU, and U.S.A.
evidence.evidenceText: Country License Status South Korea 제인 21-4042 호 EU Certi No. 0068/QCO-DM/308-2021 U.S.A. K213497

evidence.claim: Sales records in the report show 1 domestic Korea sale in 2021 and 8 overseas sales in 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Countries 2021 2022 Domestic (KOREA) 1 0 Oversea 0 8

evidence.claim: Customer complaint analysis for 2021 and 2022 reported zero safety, performance, malfunction/repair, labelling, and packaging complaints.
evidence.evidenceText: Year Safety Performance Malfunction/ Repair Labelling Packaging 2021 0 0 0 0 0 2022 0 0 0 0 0 Total 0 0 0 0 0

evidence.claim: The report states no device modifications, new products, new intended purpose, new claims, or new events since the last clinical evaluation report.
evidence.evidenceText: Whether the device has been modified N/A ... New intended purpose N/A ... New claims N/A ... New events related to the device with an impact on clinical evaluation N/A

evidence.claim: The report states equivalence was demonstrated according to MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4 using clinical, technical, and biological characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: For assuming equivalence, equivalence can only be based on a single device; ... all three characteristics (clinical, technical, biological) need to be fulfilled;

evidence.claim: The report uses DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, and S4335AW as clinical evaluation or similar device data.
evidence.evidenceText: We used as the clinical evaluation data the follow device, -DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, S4335AW

evidence.claim: The literature search used PubMed and ScienceDirect.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” were utilized to search for literature and to identify any published scientific data

evidence.claim: The literature search date range was 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
The report states no pre-market clinical investigations or PMCF studies were held by the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: All pre-market clinical investigations N/A - PMCF studies, such as post market clinical investigations and any device registries sponsored by the manufacturer N/A -

evidence.claim: Manufacturer-held PMS-related documents listed include Post Market Surveillance Plan A-PMS-003 and Periodic Safety Update Report A-PSUR-004.
evidence.evidenceText: the literature search and evaluation reports for PMS A Post Market Surveillance Plan (Doc. No. A-PMS-003) Periodic Safety Update Report (Doc. No. A-PSUR-004)

evidence.claim: The report concludes available clinical data are suitable for use based on suitability and contribution appraisal.
evidence.evidenceText: Above clinical data is suitable for use as clinical data about the result of the evaluation based on the suitability and the contribution.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the device can be expected to exhibit claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Considering the literature review, safety & performance test and comparison to similar devices, we concluded that this device can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.

evidence.claim: The report concludes serious side effects were not found in similar devices and residual risk was acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the clinical data, the serious side effects is not found in the similar device. As well as the overall residual risk has been proven to be acceptable through data analysis sufficiently and risk management

evidence.claim: The next clinical evaluation period listed after 2022.12.01 runs to 2024.12.01.
evidence.evidenceText: The next clinical evaluation 2022.12.01 ~ 2024.12.01 CE Surveillance audit

reviewFlags.reason: Source path metadata says rev.1, while the source text states Report No. A-TCF-5-060 (Rev.2).
Authority signal is general and the document identifies jurisdictions and directives rather than a single issuing authority.
The source contains internal inconsistencies or typographical issues, including '#005 Literature' followed by '#004 Literature' and references to both S4335AW and S4336AW.
Some sales/PMS sections state 'There is no sales record during this period' while another table reports sales in 2021 and 2022; period boundaries are not always explicit.
